title: Create shared cite-line parser lib (parseCiteLine → cite_ids + cite_tags + cite_commitments)
description: Extract cite-line parsing into a shared zero-dep pure-function module. Parses `KB: <id> (<anchor>) [<tag>] → <contract>` and `KB: none [<reason>]` forms. Output shape matches event-ledger schema (cite_ids[] + cite_tags[] + cite_commitments[] index-aligned). Tolerates whitespace, CR/LF, multiple cite lines per turn. Three client hooks (TASK-04) will import + inline-bundle this module to avoid node_modules dependency at hook runtime.

files.change:
Export function parseCiteLine(raw: string): { cite_ids: string[]; cite_tags: CiteTag[]; cite_commitments: CiteCommitment[] }. Match regex:
- /^KB:\s*none\s*(?:\[([^\]]*)\])?\s*$/i for sentinel
- /^KB:\s+(K[TP]-[A-Z]+-\d+)(?:\s+\(([^)]+)\))?(?:\s+\[([^\]]+)\])?(?:\s+→\s*(.+))?$/ for full cite
Contract tail parser:
- Split contract string by whitespace into tokens
- Each token matches /^(!?edit|require|forbid):(.+)$/ → operator {kind, target}
- Or /^skip:(\S+)$/ → skip_reason
- Tokens that don't match any → ignored (forward-compat)
Return structured arrays, all index-aligned. Module is zero-dep TypeScript, exports type CiteTag = 'planned'|'recalled'|'chained-from'|'dismissed'|'none' and type CiteCommitment = { operators: Array<{kind, target}>, skip_reason: string|null }.

Cover cases: (1) bare KB: none; (2) KB: none [no-relevant]; (3) KB: KT-DEC-0001 (Summary) [planned]; (4) full form with contract: 'KB: KT-DEC-9003 (Summary) [recalled] → edit:.fabric/AGENTS.md !edit:CLAUDE.md'; (5) skip form: '→ skip:sequencing'; (6) multiple operators mixed; (7) malformed contract token ignored gracefully; (8) CR/LF tolerance; (9) leading whitespace tolerance; (10) multiple cite lines (returns multiple cite_ids/tags/commitments). At least 15 cases.
Add `export { parseCiteLine } from './cite-line-parser'; export type { CiteTag, CiteCommitment, CiteCommitmentOperator } from './cite-line-parser';`.

implementation: Create packages/shared/src/cite-line-parser.ts as zero-dep module (no other imports allowed — hook runtime constraint).
Define types: CiteTag union, CiteCommitmentOperator object, CiteCommitment object.
Implement parseCiteLine function: split input by newlines, parse each non-empty line, accumulate results.
Implement parseContractTail helper: tokenize by whitespace, classify each token via regex, collect into operators[] + skip_reason.
Add comprehensive test file covering all enumerated cases.
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared test cite-line-parser` to confirm all green.
Verify module is zero-dep: `grep -E '^import' packages/shared/src/cite-line-parser.ts` should return empty (only type-only imports allowed).

reference.pattern: Zero-dep pure-function utility module (consumable by hook runtime)
reference.examples: Existing rc.20 inline parser in fabric-hint.cjs (target for extraction). No prior pattern for shared lib consumed by hook — TASK-03 establishes it.

rationale.chosen_approach: Extract parser to shared lib; three hooks become thin import + serialize wrappers. Reduces drift risk (single canonical implementation).
alternatives_considered: Duplicate parser per-client (rejected: 3x drift surface, rc.20 already had this pain)
Server-side parsing only (rejected: hook is the only component that sees per-turn cite line)
decision_factors: Future operator additions touch one file
rationale.tradeoffs: Hook bundling complexity in TASK-04 (must inline-serialize since node_modules unavailable at hook runtime).

manual_checks: Manually parse 3 real cite lines from werewolf project knowledge corpus
Verify zero external imports in module
success_metrics: ≥15 test cases covering all syntax variants
Module compiles in isolation (no monorepo type leakage)

criteria: packages/shared/src/cite-line-parser.ts has zero non-type imports (grep '^import' returns only type-only)
Commit msg: 'feat(rc24): shared cite-line parser with contract syntax support (TASK-03)'
